choices.text: Low-Rank Adaptation — adds small adapter matrices to each layer; trains only 0.1-1% of parameters while keeping base frozen. Configure via LoraConfig: rank (r), alpha, target_modules. QLoRA combines 4-bit quantized base + LoRA = fine-tune 70B on single GPU. Standard for efficient fine-tuning 2026.

questions.explanation: Low-Rank Adaptation. Trains 0.1-1% of params via small adapter matrices. QLoRA = 4-bit + LoRA = 70B on single GPU.

choices.text: QLoRA. Load base in 4-bit (NF4 quantization) = ~35 GB → too much for 24 GB alone but with CPU offloading via Accelerate fits. Add LoRA adapter via PEFT (only train ~0.1% of params). Train. Save tiny adapter (~100-500 MB). Inference: load 4-bit base + adapter. The standard way to fine-tune 70B on consumer hardware. Practical sweet spot: 13-30B for solid 24 GB workflow without offloading.

questions.explanation: QLoRA = 4-bit quantized base + LoRA adapter. Fits 70B on 24 GB with offloading. SOTA for consumer fine-tuning.
